prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(define_schema_entity schema_component_user_profile)
(reserve_storage_backend_for_entity schema_component_user_profile storage_backend_pool_a)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity schema_component_user_profile attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_attribute_attachment_to_entity schema_component_user_profile attribute_spec_1)
(claim_compliance_marker_for_component schema_component_user_profile compliance_marker_gdpr attribute_spec_1)
(assign_configurator_to_entity schema_component_user_profile configurator_agent_1)
(approve_component_compliance_stage schema_component_user_profile configurator_agent_1)
(attach_approval_signature_to_component schema_component_user_profile approval_signature_lead)
(apply_approval_signature_and_mark_compliance_ready schema_component_user_profile approval_signature_lead)
(finalize_compliance_stage_and_mark_component_releasable schema_component_user_profile)
(assign_version_token_to_schema_entity schema_component_user_profile version_token_v1 attribute_spec_1)
(commit_allocation_to_component schema_component_user_profile storage_backend_pool_a version_token_v1)
(detach_attribute_from_entity schema_component_user_profile attribute_spec_1)
(remove_approval_signature_from_component schema_component_user_profile approval_signature_lead)
(unassign_configurator_from_entity schema_component_user_profile configurator_agent_1)
(define_schema_entity schema_component_ordering)
(reserve_storage_backend_for_entity schema_component_ordering storage_backend_pool_a)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity schema_component_ordering attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_attribute_attachment_to_entity schema_component_ordering attribute_spec_1)
(claim_compliance_marker_for_component schema_component_ordering compliance_marker_pci attribute_spec_1)
(assign_configurator_to_entity schema_component_ordering configurator_agent_1)
(approve_component_compliance_stage schema_component_ordering configurator_agent_1)
(attach_approval_signature_to_component schema_component_ordering approval_signature_lead)
(apply_approval_signature_and_mark_compliance_ready schema_component_ordering approval_signature_lead)
(finalize_compliance_stage_and_mark_component_releasable schema_component_ordering)
(assign_version_token_to_schema_entity schema_component_ordering version_token_v1 attribute_spec_1)
(commit_allocation_to_component schema_component_ordering storage_backend_pool_a version_token_v1)
(detach_attribute_from_entity schema_component_ordering attribute_spec_1)
(remove_approval_signature_from_component schema_component_ordering approval_signature_lead)
(unassign_configurator_from_entity schema_component_ordering configurator_agent_1)
(define_schema_entity primary_instance_alpha)
(reserve_storage_backend_for_entity primary_instance_alpha storage_backend_pool_b)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_attribute_attachment_to_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(reserve_shard_for_primary_instance primary_instance_alpha shard_spec_1 attribute_spec_1)
(assign_configurator_to_entity primary_instance_alpha configurator_agent_1)
(prepare_primary_instance_shard_allocation primary_instance_alpha shard_spec_1 configurator_agent_1)
(define_schema_entity secondary_instance_alpha)
(reserve_storage_backend_for_entity secondary_instance_alpha storage_backend_pool_c)
(detach_attribute_from_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity secondary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_attribute_attachment_to_entity secondary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(reserve_replica_for_secondary_instance secondary_instance_alpha replica_spec_1 attribute_spec_1)
(unassign_configurator_from_entity primary_instance_alpha configurator_agent_1)
(assign_configurator_to_entity secondary_instance_alpha configurator_agent_1)
(prepare_secondary_replica_with_configurator secondary_instance_alpha replica_spec_1 configurator_agent_1)
(create_deployment_package_from_prepared_specs primary_instance_alpha secondary_instance_alpha shard_spec_1 replica_spec_1 deployment_package_initial)
(detach_attribute_from_entity secondary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(finalize_deployment_package_before_composition deployment_package_initial primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(publish_primary_instance_with_package primary_instance_alpha deployment_package_initial)
(publish_secondary_instance_with_package secondary_instance_alpha deployment_package_initial)
(assign_version_token_to_schema_entity primary_instance_alpha version_token_v1 attribute_spec_1)
(commit_allocation_to_primary_instance primary_instance_alpha storage_backend_pool_b version_token_v1)
(detach_attribute_from_entity primary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(attach_attribute_spec_to_entity secondary_instance_alpha attribute_spec_1)
(assign_version_token_to_schema_entity secondary_instance_alpha version_token_v1 attribute_spec_1)
(commit_allocation_to_secondary_instance secondary_instance_alpha storage_backend_pool_c version_token_v1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem is a classical planning problem in PDDL for data storage schema design. I need to output only the executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions, one per line, in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).


## Understanding the Problem  
- The task is a classical planning problem for designing a data storage schema using PDDL.  
- The goal is to output an executable plan as a sequence of ground PDDL actions.  

## Structuring the Output  
- Each action must be listed one per line in the format (action-name arg1 arg2 ...).  
- The output should consist solely of ground PDDL actions, without additional explanation.

## Structuring the Output  
- Schema entities like primary_instance_alpha and schema_component_user_profile need to be defined first using define_schema_entity, as they aren’t created initially.  
- Each action in the plan must ensure preconditions like not_created and not_committed are met before proceeding.  

## Exploring Entity Creation  
- Entities require multiple steps before committing allocation, including creation, validation, and backend reservation.  
- Actions like reserve_storage_backend_for_entity and assign_version_token_to_schema_entity are key to preparing entities for commitment.  

## Planning the Sequence  
- The process involves marking entities as released via actions like publish_instance_with_package, which enables version stamping.  
- The goal is to commit allocation for all four entities, requiring a sequence that balances interdependent actions like attaching attributes and assigning configurators.

## Managing Limited Resources  
- Only one attribute specification (attr_spec_1) is available, but all four entities need attribute_attached for various actions, including validation and version stamping.  
- Entities can attach, validate with the attribute, then detach it, allowing reuse for other entities to meet requirements like schema_entity_attribute_attached.  

## Handling Shared Dependencies  
- Other limited resources include one configurator_agent, one deployment_package, and one version_token, requiring careful sequencing to ensure availability.  
- Data assets, compliance, labels, and access policies (two each) need proper association, while shard and replica specifications (one each) must align with entity needs.  

## Sequencing for Release  
- Marking entities as released can happen via publish_primary_instance_with_package for instances or finalize_component_for_release for components, requiring prior preparation like staging_complete.  
- Version stamping requires marked_released, attribute_attached, and an available token, necessitating a sequence that balances these interdependent states.  

## Planning for Commitment  
- Committing allocation needs reserved_storage_backend, obtained via reserve_storage_backend_for_entity, ensuring entities are created and backend is available.  
- The plan must manage shared resources and ensure all preconditions, like validation and release marking, are met for the four entities.

## Exploring Release Paths  
- Components can mark released via finalize_component_for_release, requiring staging_complete and not being promotion_ready.  
- For instances, marking released likely depends on publish_primary_instance_with_package, needing prepared state and a finalized package.  

## Navigating Compliance Routes  
- Components can use a compliance path like claim_compliance_marker_for_component, requiring validated status and available marker, leading to marked_released without package dependency.  
- Instances, belonging to schema_entity_group_a, lack compliance actions, limiting release options to package-related paths.  

## Managing Circular Dependencies  
- Finalizing a deployment package requires a prepared primary with attribute attached, but attributes may need detaching for reuse, complicating sequencing.  
- Data asset bundling for components involves attach_data_asset_to_component_and_package, but package readiness creates potential circular dependencies with component validation.

## Sequencing Component Release  
- The process for releasing a component, like schema_component_user_profile, starts with validation and reserving a backend from pool_c.  
- Attach attribute spec (attr_spec_1), claim compliance marker (e.g., GDPR), and assign configurator (configurator_agent_1) to move through compliance stages.  

- Attach approval signature (approval_signature_lead) to finalize compliance, ensuring it's available and the component is validated.  
- Finalize the compliance stage to mark the component as released, without needing data assets or packages, simplifying the path.  

## Managing Shared Resources  
- Approval signature (approval_signature_lead) and version token (version_token_v1) are single-use, requiring removal or reassignment after use, like removing signature post-commit.  
- Backends are limited (three total), so completing and committing one component frees resources for the next, managing pool_c, pool_a, and pool_b.  

## Planning for Multiple Components  
- To release multiple components, sequence actions: complete one, commit to free the backend and token, then start the next, avoiding resource conflicts.  
- Ensure attributes remain attached post-compliance for version stamping, and plan for instances needing separate paths later.

## Handling Instance Preparation  
- To prepare secondary_instance_alpha, first detach attribute_spec_1 from primary_instance_alpha and attach it to the secondary.  
- Reserve a storage backend from pool_c for secondary_instance_alpha and unassign configurator_agent_1 from primary to assign to secondary, ensuring readiness for packaging.  

## Finalizing Deployment Package  
- Create a deployment package by combining prepared primary and secondary instances, ensuring both are validated, with shards and replicas reserved.  
- To finalize the package, detach attribute_spec_1 from secondary_instance_alpha and reattach to primary_instance_alpha, then mark the package as finalized.  

## Releasing Instances  
- Publish both primary_instance_alpha and secondary_instance_alpha with the finalized deployment package, marking them as released without needing attribute attachment for secondary.  
- After release, attribute_spec_1 remains attached to primary_instance_alpha, and configurator_agent_1 is last assigned to secondary_instance_alpha.  

## Managing Version Stamping  
- Assign version_token_v1 to primary_instance_alpha for version stamping, requiring it to be marked_released and attribute attached.  
- Commit the allocation for primary_instance_alpha using storage_backend_pool_b and version_token_v1, completing the process.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
